tax: Placed at order rank, Fluvial Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a liquid-water body, not ice or vapour, bound to a water source. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct liquid-water mode with its own source and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a standing water column or a churning foam-body where water should spread or fall normally. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a windless standing wave, scoured plunge-pool, or high-water mark — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Fluvial Elementals occupies sumps, pools, cascades, and falls. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure animates still or moving water at a source and recur with the sump refills or the fall keeps running, sustaining themselves on water-pressure or the kinetic energy of falling water.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members hide as ordinary water and rouse to intrusion, drowning by surge or battering by churn. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a coherent liquid body, immunity to cutting, and a freezing or draining failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: surging, drowning, sweeping, and spray. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Fluvial Elementals matters because they make every sump and fall a drowning trap. It lets survey reports sort water hazards by motion, so a crew knows whether to sound a pool or rope below a fall without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Fluvial Elementals is the order-rank grouping of water elementals whose bodies are liquid water, still or moving within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of coherent liquid water bound to a water source, dependent on it for mass, and divide below into family groups by whether the water is still and deep or aerated and falling. Fluvial elementals are the Stillwardens' chief concern, since they hide in every sump and fall.
